behavior: Active canopy and edge forager; gleans insects, sallies after flying prey, and shifts to berries in colder seasons.
field_marks: Bright yellow rump, conspicuous in flight
Small yellow patch on each side of the breast
Full-bodied warbler with a relatively large head and long, narrow tail
White wing bars or patches and white tail spots
Gray to brown-streaked plumage with brighter yellow in breeding birds; throat may be white or yellow among forms
habitat: Open coniferous and mixed woodland, forest edges, bogs, and other wooded openings; outside the breeding season also open woods, brushy thickets, and shrub-rich habitats.
